word_list.pronunciation: æz klˈiːn æz ɐ nˈuː pˈɪn

senses.definition: kept in a very clean condition

examples.x: After the thorough spring cleaning, the house was as clean as a new pin, with every surface gleaming and organized.
The mechanic ensured that the engine was clean as a pin before putting it back together.
She always keeps her desk clean as a new pin, with papers neatly filed and everything in its proper place.
The hotel room was spotless and clean as a pin when they checked in, creating a welcoming and comfortable environment.
Despite having a busy schedule, she managed to keep her car as clean as a pin, regularly washing and detailing it to maintain its pristine appearance.

senses.definition: completely unstained and free from dirt, thus extremely clean and tidy

examples.x: After the thorough scrubbing, the kitchen was clean as a whistle, with every surface shining and devoid of any grease or stains.
The detective found the crime scene clean as a whistle, without any traces of evidence that could help solve the case.
He took great pride in his grooming and appearance, always ensuring he looked clean as a whistle before leaving the house.
The company's financial records were audited and found to be clean as a whistle, indicating transparency and adherence to regulations.
Despite the muddy trail, the hiker's boots were clean as a whistle, thanks to her diligent efforts in wiping them after each step.
She always keeps her wardrobe in apple-pie order, with clothes neatly folded and arranged by color.
The books on the shelf were arranged in apple-pie order, making it easy to find any title at a glance.
After the renovation, the living room was in apple-pie order, with furniture arranged perfectly and everything in its designated place.
The teacher expects the students to keep their desks in apple-pie order, ensuring that all materials are organized and tidy.
Despite the busy schedule, he managed to keep his workspace in apple-pie order, with papers filed neatly and a clutter-free environment.

senses.definition: in a  disorganized and untidy way

examples.x: Her presentation was confusing; she jumped from one topic to another, and her ideas were all over the place.
The team's performance was inconsistent; they were all over the place, struggling to maintain a steady level of play.
His writing style was disjointed, with ideas and examples thrown in randomly, making the essay feel all over the place.
The party planning was chaotic; decorations were placed haphazardly, and the music playlist was all over the place, lacking a cohesive theme.
The meeting was disorganized, with discussions jumping from topic to topic and ideas being thrown all over the place.
Her thoughts were all over the place during the exam, making it challenging to concentrate.
The team's performance was inconsistent, with their passes going all over the place and lacking coordination.

senses.definition: to rid something of dirt in a rapid manner

examples.x: He glanced at the report, giving it a once-over to check for any obvious errors before submitting it.

senses.definition: used to refer to someone or something that is very tidy and poperly arranged

examples.x: Despite the rainy weather, she looked nice as ninepence, with her hair perfectly styled and her outfit immaculate.
The garden was maintained with great care, always looking as nice as ninepence with its well-trimmed hedges and vibrant flowers.
The shop display was arranged beautifully, with products neatly arranged and the shelves looking as nice as ninepence.
Even after a long day at work, she ensured her desk was tidy and organized, keeping it as nice as ninepence.
The bed and breakfast was known for its impeccable cleanliness, with every room looking as nice as ninepence for the guests' arrival.

senses.definition: (of something) not positioned in its usual place

examples.x: The vibrant painting looked out of place in the otherwise minimalist and monochromatic room.
His casual attire seemed out of place at the formal event, where most people were dressed in elegant suits and gowns.
The modern sculpture appeared out of place in the historical museum, surrounded by classical artworks and artifacts.
The joke he made during the serious meeting was completely out of place and drew awkward reactions from the attendees.
The worn-out furniture seemed out of place in the newly renovated and stylishly decorated living room.

senses.definition: a person or thing that is marked by untidiness

examples.x: His desk was a dog's breakfast, cluttered with papers, pens, and random objects.
The children's playroom looked like a dog's dinner, with toys strewn all over the floor.
The gardener's unkempt appearance and disheveled clothes made him look like a dog's breakfast.
After a long day of work, the living room became a dog's dinner with discarded clothes and scattered belongings.
The project's timeline was a dog's breakfast, with missed deadlines and inconsistent progress.

senses.definition: in a state of extreme excitement or confusion

examples.x: When she won the lottery, she was head over heels with excitement and couldn't believe her good fortune.
The children ran head over heels into the amusement park, eager to experience the thrill of the rides.
After receiving the promotion, he was head over heels with joy as it was a significant milestone in his career.
The surprise announcement left everyone in the room head over heels, trying to process the unexpected news.
The chaotic scene at the concert had the crowd head over heels, with people pushing and jostling to get closer to the stage.

senses.definition: used to refer to something that has been kept in a very clean and tidy state

examples.x: After a good night's rest, she woke up feeling fresh as a daisy, ready to start her day.
The newly painted room looked fresh as a daisy with its clean walls and polished furniture.
Despite the long journey, the travelers arrived at their destination looking fresh as a daisy, thanks to their careful grooming.
The laundry was done and put away, leaving the wardrobe fresh as a daisy with neatly folded clothes.
The chef presented the dish with pristine plating, making it look as fresh as a daisy.
